DBA KELLER WILLIAMS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist whether the complained-of acts were undertaken by the agent in the scope of her employment by the broker in relation to the real estate transaction it issue in this action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KARIN MENG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL VS. LLOYD T. WEST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2023 line 2. CROSS-DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for indemnity, contribution, declaratory relief and unjust enrichment. These claims are sufficiently stated that the pleading stage regarding causes of action sounding in negligence asserted by the plaintiff in this action. The applicability of the express indemnity clause to the facts of the cross-complaint, as alleged, cannot be determined as this stage of the litigation. Demurrer to other causes of action is sustained with leave to amend for the Cross-Complainant to either attach the contract or quote the breached provision verbatim; to allege the facts supporting breach of the provisions and to allege accrued damages. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VERPRESAR, LLC VS. 1901 PACIFIC LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2023 line 4. SUPERIOR COURT RECEIVER KEVIN SINGER'S AMENDED NOTICE OF HEARING RE: SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Moving party carried its burden under the first prong of the Anti-Slapp. Defendant's motion was timely filed on June 9, 2023 (re-noticed on June 30, 2023) in accordance with April 7, 2023 service date (Singh Declaration, paragraph 15). The complaint subject to this motion was served, but not properly filed. On April 8, 2023 Plaintiff submitted a stipulation without a proposed order (not following the "Stipulation and order" procedure regarding the filing to the complaint). On April 24, 2023 the clerk rejected the filing with instructions to obtain a court order. Plaintiff appears to have followed those instructions months later, on July 24, 2023 by presenting an ex-parte application to file a Corrected First Amended Complaint pursuant to a stipulation. Had Plaintiff timely availed itself of the available procedures for obtaining an order (stipulation and order, ex-parte or motion) the procedural issue regarding serving, but not filing a Corrected First Amended Complaint, could have been avoided or resolved well in advance of the hearing and the briefing deadlines. The issue of the Corrected First Amended Complaint was resolved by July 24, 2023 ex-parte and the Court allows the filing of the Corrected First Amended Complaint per stipulation of the parties nunc pro tunc to the original date of the filing of the stipulation, i.e. April 8, 2023. Late-filed opposition is stricken. All the arguments made in the late-filed opposition could have been presented 9 court days before the hearing even with the procedural posture of the case created and not rectified by the Plaintiff starting with the filing of a wrong complaint on April 3, 2023 until July 24, 2023. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 200 PAUL, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY PROCEEDINGS is DENIED. There is no binding arbitration clause covering this dispute. While Section 3.3.1 references "mediation or arbitration before resorting to the filing of suit," there is no requirement to proceed with the disputes in arbitration only. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
